draft.introduction: The Ifa divination system of the Yoruba people is one of the most sophisticated knowledge traditions in Africa. The 256 Odu (sacred verses) encode cosmology, ethics, history, and prophecy in oral poetic form.

translation.text: Eji Ogbe, the first and highest of the Odu, speaks: The world was created in perfect order. The right hand knows the left. The farmer who plants in season reaps abundance. The one who consults Ifa before acting will not stumble. Orunmila, witness to creation, saw all that would unfold before it began. He said: truth is the character of the Almighty. Let the wise align themselves with truth, for the crooked path leads to the house of regret.

translator_notes.note: Orunmila is the Yoruba deity of wisdom and divination who witnessed the creation of the world. The Ifa system's 256 Odu are generated by binary combinations (open and closed marks), making it a mathematical-divinatory system that independently discovered binary notation centuries before Leibniz. UNESCO recognized Ifa as an Intangible Cultural Heritage of Humanity in 2005.
